# Partial splits of const blocks with implicit expressions are rejected.
# B and C inherit A's expression, so selecting only B would be misleading.

! exec sflit -source big.go -sink small.go -regex '^B$' -move
stderr 'cannot partially split const block with implicit expressions'
cmp big.go expected_big.go
! exists small.go

# Copy mode (no -move) is rejected too: the sink would contain a bare
# `const B` — invalid Go.
! exec sflit -source big.go -sink copy.go -regex '^B$'
stderr 'cannot partially split const block with implicit expressions'
cmp big.go expected_big.go
! exists copy.go

# Moving the whole implicit const block is allowed.
exec sflit -source big.go -sink all.go -regex '^(A|B|C)$' -move
cmp all.go expected_all.go

-- big.go --
package p

const (
	A = "x"
	B
	C
)
-- expected_big.go --
package p

const (
	A = "x"
	B
	C
)
-- expected_all.go --
package p

const (
	A = "x"
	B
	C
)
